title: Cron Unschedule Bug
prompt: A plugin schedules a daily sync on activation but never clears it. Write the fix plan: scheduling guard, callback boundaries, deactivation cleanup, and checks that the job is idempotent.
hints: Scheduled work should be bounded, repeat-safe, and removed when the plugin is deactivated.

model_answer.summary: The fix is to schedule once, make the callback repeat-safe, and remove the event during deactivation.
model_answer.work: On activation, call wp_next_scheduled before wp_schedule_event so the daily cron event is not duplicated. Keep the cron callback bounded: process a small batch, use a short lock or transient to avoid overlap, and make every record update idempotent so reruns do not duplicate remote writes. Register the callback on a named hook and keep remote API, storage, and logging boundaries clear. On deactivation, call wp_clear_scheduled_hook for that hook so the plugin leaves no orphaned scheduled work.
checks: Verify activation creates only one scheduled event.
Run the cron callback twice and confirm it is idempotent.
Use WP-CLI to list cron events before and after deactivation.
Smoke test lock expiry and batch limits.
Test remote API failure handling without leaving the job stuck.
model_answer.why_this_is_good: It fixes both lifecycle cleanup and the deeper reliability issue: background work must tolerate retries.
common_misses: Scheduling on every load instead of activation.
Clearing the wrong hook name during deactivation.
Assuming the callback only ever runs once.
model_answer.revision_prompt: Revise by naming the scheduling guard, cron hook, deactivation cleanup, idempotent callback behavior, and verification commands.
